claim: For all integers n > 1, sigma(n) is at least n + 1, with equality exactly when n is prime.
If n is composite, then sigma(n) > n + 1.
For all primes p, sigma(p) = p + 1.
If n is composite, then sigma(n) >= n + 1 + min(proper_divisors(n)).

claim: For all primes p, sigma(p) - p = 1.
For all n > 1, sigma(n) / n > 1.
For composite n, sigma(n) / n > (n + 2) / n.

If n is a perfect square composite, then sigma(n) >= 2n.

n is prime if and only if delta(n) = n - 1.

claim: sigma(n) is odd if and only if n is a perfect square or twice a perfect square.
For odd prime p, sigma(p) = p + 1 is even.

phi(n) < sigma(n) for all n > 1.

For n > 1, the sum of proper divisors of n is at least 1.
